title: Orobanche dugesii
intro: Orobanche dugesii, commonly known as Dugès' broomrape, is a parasitic plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Orobanche dugesii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions, as well as to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Orobanche dugesii is a species within the genus Orobanche, which belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This genus is commonly known as the broomrape family. Orobanche dugesii is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting with the roots of other plants. The classification of Orobanche dugesii is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Lamiales, family Orobanchaceae, genus Orobanche, species O. dugesii. This classification indicates its relationships to other plants within the kingdom.
